chunk_summary: Guidelines for weaning and stopping post-discharge breast milk fortifier in preterm/LBW/ELBW infants emphasize proportional growth, tapering schedules, and clear stop criteria to ensure safe and effective nutrition transitions.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of proportional growth in determining when to wean or stop breast milk fortifier post-discharge.
Learn how to implement a step-down schedule for fortifier supplementation with reassessment intervals.
Recognize signs of overnutrition or disproportionate growth and adjust fortification accordingly.
Develop clear documentation and follow-up plans for post-fortification nutrition strategies.
key_facts: Weaning plans should taper fortifier doses over weeks, e.g., from 6/day to 4/day to 2/day.
Fortification should continue only if weight gain is appropriate over 2–3 weeks with no down-crossing of growth curves.
Stop fortification if weight-for-length exceeds the 85th percentile to avoid overnutrition.
Reassess nutrition plans if weight gain is disproportionate to length or head circumference.
Document the stop endpoint and next nutrition plan to ensure caregiver clarity.
reference_quote: The ABM protocol uses >85th percentile as a practical trigger to reduce supplementation to avoid overnutrition.
document_key_topics: Proportional growth as a criterion for weaning
Clear documentation and follow-up nutrition plans
